# AgentsPay

> AgentsPay is a local MCP server that gives Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, and Zed agents a budget-controlled USDC wallet for x402-priced APIs. Per-call and daily caps are enforced before signing; every settlement is recorded in SQLite and verifiable on Solana.

AgentsPay distributes as a single Rust binary (`agentspay-mcp`) that an MCP host installs locally. It speaks the Model Context Protocol over stdio and exposes five tools to the agent: `agentspay_balance`, `agentspay_pay_url`, `agentspay_set_budget`, `agentspay_audit_log`, `agentspay_topup_info`. The product status is v0.3 on Solana devnet; mainnet is gated behind a v0.5 compliance review.

The wedge: agent developers want their agent to autonomously pay for x402-priced APIs without handing it a credit card or an unrestricted keypair. AgentsPay enforces per-call and daily USDC spend caps *before* signing — the agent cannot drain the wallet. Every paid call writes an audit row in SQLite.

## Core links

- [Home](https://agentspay.dev/): Project overview, install command, live proof.
- [Demo](https://agentspay.dev/demo): Browser sandbox and a one-click button that signs a real Solana devnet USDC transfer through the same `agentspay-mcp` binary.
- [Proof](https://agentspay.dev/proof): Solscan-confirmed settlement ledger with permanent records plus the latest browser-triggered transaction.
- [Docs](https://agentspay.dev/docs): Tool reference, payment flow, budgets, devnet funding, security model.

## Code

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/h4ckm1n/agentspay): MIT-licensed Rust + Next.js source.
- [Releases](https://github.com/h4ckm1n/agentspay/releases): Pre-built binaries for macOS / Linux / Windows.

## How an agent uses AgentsPay

1. The user installs `agentspay-mcp` and registers it with their MCP host (`claude mcp add agentspay $PWD/target/release/agentspay-mcp`).
2. First run writes a self-custodial keypair to `~/.agentspay/keypair.json` (mode 0600) and creates the SQLite ledger.
3. The user funds the agent pubkey with devnet SOL and devnet USDC.
4. The user sets a budget once: `agentspay_set_budget(daily_usd=25, per_call_usd=1)`.
5. From then on, when the agent encounters an x402-priced URL it calls `agentspay_pay_url(url, max_amount)`. AgentsPay checks the budget, signs a Solana SPL `transfer_checked`, retries the request with the `X-Payment` header, and writes a ledger entry. The agent receives the response body.

## Architecture, briefly

- `services/mcp/` — `agentspay-mcp` binary (Rust, rmcp 0.16, Tokio, SeaORM, SQLite).
- `services/web-shim/` — Axum HTTP bridge that wraps `agentspay-mcp` stdio for the website demo.
- `examples/paid-endpoint/` — Demo x402 provider used by the demo button.
- `apps/frontend/` — Next.js 15 marketing site.
- `docker/` — 5-service compose stack for the public demo (Caddy + web + shim + paid-endpoint + Redis).

## Settlement

USDC on Solana via the official x402 protocol. Devnet only in v0.3. Real settlements complete in ~2 seconds at ~$0.00025 per tx. Three permanent receipt examples on the [proof page](https://agentspay.dev/proof).

## Non-goals (v0.1 through v0.3)

No auth or API key service (the MCP host is the auth boundary). No gRPC. No REST mutation API. No public TypeScript or Python SDK. No webhook delivery. No multi-tenant. No KYC. No mainnet money. No dashboard UI. The web-shim is a demo backend, not a hosted service.
